skill.name: github-actions-docs
skill.description: Provides documentation-grounded help for GitHub Actions workflows, syntax, runners, security, deployments, custom actions, and migrations using official GitHub docs.
skill.summary: Finds the right official GitHub Actions documentation for workflow guidance.
skill.author: xixu-me

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged command execution, token access, weak cryptography, and a dangerous combination heuristic. Manual review found only Markdown guidance, inline code formatting, and official GitHub documentation links, with no executable code, secret reads, exfiltration flow, or prompt injection. The remaining risk is low because the skill intentionally directs assistants to access docs.github.com for current GitHub Actions documentation.

low_findings.title: Official Documentation Network Dependency
low_findings.description: The skill instructs assistants to use docs.github.com and includes many official GitHub documentation links. This is expected for a documentation lookup skill, but it depends on external network access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network behavior is explicit and limited to official GitHub documentation URLs. No non-GitHub endpoints or hidden data transfer instructions were found.

low_findings.title: False Positive Command Execution Matches
low_findings.description: Static command execution matches are Markdown inline-code references to tools, phrases, or files, not shell execution. The reviewed files contain no scripts or runnable command blocks.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review shows plain Markdown prose and inline code spans only. There is no code file, shell invocation, subprocess API, or executable payload.

low_findings.title: False Positive Credential Access Matches
low_findings.description: The GITHUB_TOKEN references describe official GitHub Actions documentation topics. They do not read environment variables, request user secrets, or transmit credentials.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The token mentions are documentation labels and link text in GitHub Actions guidance. No semantic evidence of credential collection or exfiltration was found.

content.user_title: Answer GitHub Actions Questions from Docs
content.value_statement: GitHub Actions guidance can become stale or too generic. This skill routes Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to official GitHub documentation for current, cited workflow answers.
actual_capabilities: Classifies GitHub Actions questions by topic before searching.
Routes users to official docs for workflow syntax, events, contexts, and expressions.
Supports guidance for runners, matrices, reusable workflows, artifacts, and caching.
Covers security topics such as secrets, GITHUB_TOKEN, OIDC, and artifact attestations.
Helps find migration documentation for Jenkins, CircleCI, GitLab CI/CD, and other systems.
Encourages cited answers with direct links to relevant GitHub documentation pages.
limitations: It does not triage a specific failing pull request check or workflow log.
It does not replace CodeQL or Dependabot focused guidance.
It depends on live GitHub documentation for the final authoritative answer.
It does not execute workflows, inspect repositories, or change GitHub settings by itself.

use_cases.title: Write a New Workflow
use_cases.description: Find the right syntax and starter documentation before creating a GitHub Actions workflow.

use_cases.title: Secure Cloud Deployments
use_cases.description: Use official docs for OIDC, secrets, GITHUB_TOKEN permissions, and deployment environments.

use_cases.title: Plan CI Migration
use_cases.description: Locate GitHub migration guides when moving pipelines from Jenkins, CircleCI, or GitLab CI/CD.

prompt_templates.title: Find the Right Docs
prompt_templates.prompt: Find the official GitHub Actions documentation for workflow triggers and explain which page I should read first.

prompt_templates.title: Explain Workflow Syntax
prompt_templates.prompt: Use official GitHub Actions docs to explain jobs, steps, contexts, and expressions for a workflow I am writing.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Deployment Security
prompt_templates.prompt: Find the GitHub Actions docs for OIDC deployments and GITHUB_TOKEN permissions, then summarize the safest documented approach.

prompt_templates.title: Map a CI Migration
prompt_templates.prompt: Use official GitHub Actions migration docs to compare Jenkins pipeline concepts with GitHub workflow concepts and list the key migration steps.

output_examples.input: How should I learn workflow triggers?
output_examples.output: Start with the Events that trigger workflows reference, then read the workflow syntax page for event configuration details.

output_examples.input: Should I store AWS keys as GitHub secrets for deployment?
output_examples.output: The skill would point to GitHub OIDC documentation and explain when short-lived cloud credentials are the documented safer option.

output_examples.input: I am moving a Jenkins pipeline to GitHub Actions.
output_examples.output: The skill would route you to the migration hub and Jenkins migration guide, then summarize the main concept mappings.

best_practices: Ask for the exact GitHub Actions topic, version concern, or workflow area you need.
Request direct GitHub documentation links when you need a source for a team decision.
Separate documentation questions from repository-specific CI debugging requests.
anti_patterns: Do not use it as a live workflow log debugger for one failing run.
Do not rely on memory-only answers when official documentation can be checked.
Do not use it for CodeQL or Dependabot topics when a focused skill is available.

faq.question: Does this skill write GitHub Actions workflow files?
faq.answer: It can help with docs-grounded workflow guidance, but examples are provided only when useful or requested.

faq.question: Which documentation source does it prefer?
faq.answer: It prefers official GitHub documentation under docs.github.com, especially the GitHub Actions section.

faq.question: Can it debug my failed workflow run?
faq.answer: It is not intended for live run triage. Use a repository or CI troubleshooting tool for that task.

faq.question: Does it cover GitHub Actions security?
faq.answer: Yes. It routes to docs for secrets, GITHUB_TOKEN, OIDC, secure use, and artifact attestations.

faq.question: Can it help with migration from another CI system?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes routing for GitHub documentation about Jenkins, CircleCI, GitLab CI/CD, and general migrations.

faq.question: Does it need internet access?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill is most useful when it can check current official GitHub documentation.
